scenarioMatrix.expected: No file is written; output says dry-run-add.
scenarioMatrix.riskCovered: Prevents accidental config mutation while evaluating an MCP server.

scenarioMatrix.expected: First apply writes one fragment, second apply without --force fails, --force replaces.
scenarioMatrix.riskCovered: Prevents hidden reinstall/duplicate drift and makes replacement explicit.

scenarioMatrix.expected: Writes command/args only; does not execute the command during add.
Accepts http(s) URL and headers as config; rejects non-http(s).
scenarioMatrix.riskCovered: Separates remote domain ownership from local MCPace endpoint ownership.

scenarioMatrix.expected: Entry can be added with enabled=false; costs remain dependent on later runtime/tool calls.
scenarioMatrix.riskCovered: Avoids accidental activation while still allowing reviewable config.

scenarioMatrix.expected: 100 distinct fragments are written and visible in source inventory.
scenarioMatrix.riskCovered: Covers many-server config fanout without claiming runtime can safely run all concurrently.

checks.detail: source-level proof: install_auto passes dry_run into write_mcp_server_entry and server install routes through mcp_autoinstall.
source-level proof: npm/PyPI/OCI specs materialize reviewable MCP settings fragments through the source writer.
source-level proof: auto install constructs command/args only and does not spawn package launchers.
source-level proof: duplicate names remain blocked by the shared MCP settings writer unless --force is passed.
source-level proof: force replacement removes the normalized existing key before writing the replacement entry.
exit=1 server add --url currently accepts only http:// or https:// MCP endpoints

observations: Auto-install source has been updated, but the vendored binary in this sandbox still predates that Rust source change; these auto-install checks are source-level until a Rust host rebuilds the binary.
server install/add writes MCP settings fragments; it does not download packages or invoke upstream tools during registration.
npx/uvx/docker-derived entries defer package fetch/cache behavior until the command is later executed by server test, runtime, or a client.

Remote URL domains are upstream domains, not owned by MCPace unless the user controls that endpoint. MCPace serve.publicUrl is the advertised MCPace endpoint and must point to a user-controlled relay/domain when set.
100 configured servers is a config-scale scenario; it does not prove safe concurrent runtime launch of 100 expensive servers.
warnings: This smoke suite verifies registration semantics, not real package install latency, provider billing behavior, or live MCP tool calls.
Run live server tests only against reviewed servers with explicit credentials and cost limits.
